headline: Bridging Claude Code to the Codex Ecosystem
lede: This plugin standardizes high-level task delegation and adversarial code reviews within your terminal. By wrapping the Codex binary, it enables secure, multi-agent collaboration on complex architectural logic.

headline: Cutting LLM Token Consumption by Two-Thirds
lede: The caveman-style prompting protocol forces models to prioritize technical density over conversational filler. This approach dramatically lowers API costs while maintaining core reasoning accuracy for coding agents.

headline: Control Web Interfaces via Natural Language
lede: Page Agent eliminates backend bloat by performing DOM manipulation entirely within the browser. It offers a lightweight, dependency-free path to automating complex SaaS and enterprise interfaces.

headline: Automating Penetration Testing at Scale
lede: Strix functions as an autonomous red team that identifies vulnerabilities and generates actionable proof-of-concept exploits. It integrates directly into CI/CD pipelines to validate security postures before production deployment.

headline: Granting AI Agents Deep Browser Visibility
lede: This MCP server bridges LLMs to Chrome internals, providing real-time access to performance traces and console logs. It enables autonomous agents to verify code against live browser behavior.

headline: Securing Meeting Transcripts on Local Hardware
lede: Meetily leverages local Rust-based processing to handle transcription and summarization without cloud dependencies. It ensures total data sovereignty for enterprise meetings and sensitive discussions.

headline: Archiving the Hidden System Instructions of Modern AI
lede: This crowdsourced repository tracks the evolving behavioral constraints defined by major AI labs. It serves as a foundational index for researchers studying model alignment and prompt architecture.

headline: Establishing a Rigorous Curriculum for AI Infrastructure
lede: The Harvard CS249r book moves beyond theoretical models to address the physical reality of AI systems. It covers the essential engineering trade-offs required for reliable, large-scale deployment.

headline: Modernizing Retro Game Library Management
lede: RomM transforms fragmented ROM collections into a sleek, metadata-rich digital storefront. It brings unified organization and instant playability to local game archives.

headline: Multiplexing AI Agents Directly in the Terminal
lede: Herdr manages concurrent agent processes through a persistent, lightweight terminal session architecture. It provides a robust command-line environment for monitoring multiple autonomous workflows simultaneously.
